description_long: This object is a large, flat solar energy installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a broad, stepped platform. The panels are arranged in a staggered configuration, with some tilted at an angle and others lying flat, suggesting optimization for sunlight capture. The platform itself appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and concrete, with raised sections and integrated support structures. Several black box-like components, likely representing inverters or electrical housings, are distributed across the surface. The overall design is industrial and robust, suitable for placement on rooftops or as a ground-mounted solar array in an urban environment. The color palette is dominated by shades of gray, blue, and black, giving it a modern, utilitarian appearance.
description: A large, stepped platform with multiple rectangular solar panels, some tilted and some flat, featuring black electrical housings and a modern industrial design.
description_view_0: From this view, the stepped platform and the tilt of the solar panels are most prominent, with black housings visible on the right side.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the linear arrangement of the panels and the full width of the platform, showing the distribution of black housings and the stepped structure.
description_view_2: Here, the tilt of the panels is clearly visible, and the raised sections of the platform are more apparent, with electrical housings distributed across the surface.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the flatness of the platform and the arrangement of the panels, with the black housings and stepped edges visible along the length.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30 degrees, with the tilt facing toward the longer edge of the platform; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with stepped concrete and metal support structure, likely aluminum frame.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows of panels in a staggered, stepped configuration, approximately 3-4 main panel groups.
